cases.prompt: /grill-me 사용자가 글 작성하면 알림 보내주는 기능 만들고 싶어

assertions.text: The agent asks exactly one question per turn and waits for the user's answer.
Each question includes 'My recommendation: X, because Y.'
Early questions clarify scope (which users get notified, in-app vs email vs push, sync vs async).
Later questions surface edge cases: what if user has 10k followers, what if the post is deleted, what if notification fails to deliver.

cases.prompt: /grill-me Stripe 결제 추가하려는데 grill해줘 (repo has existing payment-service module)

assertions.text: The agent reads the existing payment-service module before asking questions about integration shape.
The agent does not ask questions that can be answered by reading the codebase (e.g., does not ask 'do you already have a payment module' when grep would reveal it).
If CONTEXT.md exists, the agent uses its glossary terms when phrasing questions.
The agent produces a final summary listing decisions resolved, decisions deferred, and open risks.
The summary points to the next skill (simon-tdd, autoplan, or implementation) based on what was resolved.
